scenario: You are working as a developer for a company that wants you to perform validation and integrate its Microsoft Dataverse data to an external database. You need to identify the pipeline stages in which to register the plug-in.
q: Which stages should you use to meet the requirements? To answer, drag the appropriate stages to the correct descriptions. Each stage may be used once, more than once, or not at all.
rows: Update the case resolution time on case closure.
Send Dataverse table data to an external API.
why: Register the plug-in in PRE-VALIDATION to check for duplicate data: pre-validation validates the data and can show an error to the user; its operations run outside the Dataverse transaction, ideal to validate/cancel early. Register in PRE-OPERATION to update the case resolution time on case closure: pre-operation lets you modify the data before the actual database transaction in the core stage. You cannot use post-operation (it comes after core operation, where the data is read-only). Register in POST-OPERATION to send Dataverse table data to an external API: you should always call an external API in async mode from plug-in code, and an asynchronous plug-in can only be registered in the post-operation stage. Do NOT use CORE OPERATION: that is where the main create/update/delete runs, and Microsoft does not allow registering a plug-in directly in this stage to avoid direct changes to the database.
